spanGrp.span: The garbage bin? We put our garbage in it
My husband wears a white shirt or my husband's shirt is white
We have a white car
Riding?
They rode in on six white camels.
When I graduated my mother’s face turned white with pride.
I don’t like this black shirt nor the black car
The sheikh of the tribe Abu ʕAli has 5 black stallions and 5 black mares
The "ḥiṣān" is a male and the "faras" is a female
Are you knowledgable on horses?
A little bit
My wife bought a pink shirt like the one you are wearing now
You took it from her, right? I will tell her
and a pink bag
What did you see? What did you get to know?
What did you get to know?
rmād-... what? "ramādi" (for the colour grey)
Grey pants, it's the same
The "sirwāl" is a thing and the "banṭālōn" is another, officially, the "sirwāl!... This is the # "sirwāl" that you would wear under, over the thing
But the girls, no, right? They would have the banṭālōn
No, the sirwāl is what one wears which isn't loose (# am I translating loose right?)
Yes, it's not loose,#, it's either see-through, or used in the house, or it's...
I like this orange shirt
The colour of soil is usually golden
No, because there is a colour that is the same as the soil colour, that is light gold mixed with beige, and light gold, it's almost the same colour as this marble, the marble outside
The colour of wood is usually wood-coloured or brown or light brown
The pastures are green in spring
The sky is blue today, like it (the sentence you said)
The tomatoes are red like your red cheeks
There are 30 students in the class, but only 13 are attending right now
We will visit you (pl.f.) in 2 months.
We will visit you (pl.f.) in 2 months.
If it's in the plural it's "azūr-kum"
No if it's plural, like girls, it is "rāḥ izūr-kum" or "rāḥ inzūr-kin"
"inzūr-kin" or rāḥ "inzūr-čin"
But we always say "rāḥ inzūr-kum" # plural
There is no quarrel between us
They arrived at midnight
She came with her daughters
